concept.math_concept: Slope as rise over run; two lines with different slopes (y = 1.75x vs y = 0.65x) model two trails of different steepness.
concept.real_world_analog: A steep mountain trail vs a gentler mountain trail. The slope of the line equals the slope of the trail.

anchor_objects.description: A single hiker figure with backpack viewed from behind, white line drawing on transparent background, technical illustration style, no facial detail, neutral silhouette suitable for compositing into mountain terrain. ~720px tall.

narration.script: A steep trail climbs sharply. A gentler trail has a smaller slope, because elevation changes more slowly along its path. We can describe each slope mathematically as rise over run — how much we climb for every step forward.

visual.primary: Camera arrives at a tall, sharp wireframe mountain peak in white linework on black. Slow push-in toward the peak. Hiker silhouette small at the base, suggesting scale.

beats.narration_span: (no new narration — visual development; previous caption persists)

visual.primary: Camera continues pushing in toward the steep peak. Wireframe particles flow upward along the slope, suggesting the climb. Hiker silhouette ascends a few steps. Caption from b1 remains on screen.

beats.narration_span: A gentler trail has a smaller slope,

visual.primary: Camera orbits right to reveal a second, lower and more rolling wireframe terrain. Both peaks now visible.

overlays.content: A gentler trail has a smaller slope,

beats.narration_span: because elevation changes more slowly along its path.

visual.primary: 3D coordinate axes fade in over the terrain. Two animated lines draw themselves from the origin: y=1.75x climbs steeply, y=0.65x climbs gently. Both labels appear as the lines settle.

overlays.content: The gentler mountain has a smaller slope because elevation changes more slowly.

beats.narration_span: We can describe each slope mathematically as rise over run —

visual.primary: Zoom into the gentler line. A right-triangle marker is drawn under the line showing rise and run as labeled segments. The slope formula appears next to it.

beats.narration_span: how much we climb for every step forward.

visual.primary: Pull back to reveal the full landscape transformed into an undulating wireframe wave. Four small text callouts fade in around the perimeter linking the concept to broader themes.

props.callouts_top_left: LINEAR EQUATIONS — Model the relationship between change in distance and elevation.
props.callouts_top_right: VISUALIZE CHANGE — Transform real-world changes into patterns we can see and analyze.
props.callouts_bottom_left: REAL-WORLD CONNECTION — Mountains, motion, and space follow patterns we can describe mathematically.
props.callouts_bottom_right: MEASURE AND PREDICT — Use slope to measure rate of change and predict what comes next.

overlays.content: LINEAR EQUATIONS — Model the relationship between change in distance and elevation.
VISUALIZE CHANGE — Transform real-world changes into patterns we can see and analyze.
REAL-WORLD CONNECTION — Mountains, motion, and space follow patterns we can describe mathematically.
MEASURE AND PREDICT — Use slope to measure rate of change and predict what comes next.

_reference_notes.source: Reconstructed from Example 1.mp4 (uploaded) — Veo-generated, 32s, 1280x720, animation runs full length (no dead frames).
_reference_notes.reconstruction_method: Audio extracted, pocketsphinx-transcribed (noisy), corrected from visible bottom-overlay captions in the source video. Math values (y=1.75x, y=0.65x, m=0.65) read directly from source frames at t=18s and t=24s.
outstanding: Re-run real Whisper on a machine that can reach HF/OpenAI CDN to confirm exact wording.
Beat boundaries are best-guess from caption appearance — refine after Whisper word-timings land.
